lines: Let's settle in before we begin. [short pause] A slow breath... [short pause] [short pause] in for four... [short pause] [short pause] hold for four... [short pause] [short pause] out for four... [short pause] [short pause] hold for four.

Again. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four.
One more. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] hold... [short pause] [short pause] out... [short pause] [short pause] hold.

Good. [short pause] You're grounded. [short pause] And today, before you step into anything, you're going to prepare. [short pause] Not with a checklist. [short pause] With something older and stronger.

Paul wrote to the church in Ephesus... [short pause] [short pause] a small community under real pressure, navigating real conflict, both visible and invisible... [short pause] [short pause] and he told them this:

"Be strong in the Lord and in his mighty power. [short pause] Put on the full armor of God, so that you can take your stand against the devil's schemes. [short pause] For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world."

This isn't metaphor for the passive. [short pause] This is preparation for someone entering the arena. [short pause] And today... [short pause] [short pause] you suit up. [short pause] Not in arrogance. [short pause] In readiness. [short pause] Piece by piece.

Start with the belt of truth. [short pause] Feel your core... [short pause] [short pause] your center of gravity. [short pause] Tighten it slightly. [short pause] Engage those muscles. [short pause] Truth is what holds everything else in place. [short pause] It's the anchor piece. [short pause] Without honesty... [short pause] [short pause] with yourself, with God, with the people around you... [short pause] [short pause] nothing else fits right. [short pause] Every other piece depends on this one.
Lord, make me honest today. [short pause] With my words, with my motives, with myself. [short pause] Where I'm tempted to pretend, give me the courage to be real.

Now the breastplate of righteousness. [short pause] Take a deep breath and feel your chest expand. [short pause] This covers your heart... [short pause] [short pause] the most vulnerable thing you carry. [short pause] And righteousness here isn't perfection. [short pause] It's right relationship. [short pause] With God. [short pause] With others. [short pause] With yourself. [short pause] Your worth isn't in your performance today. [short pause] It never was. [short pause] It's in whose you are.
Lord, guard my heart. [short pause] When I'm tempted to earn my worth through output, remind me that my identity is already secure in you.

Your feet. [short pause] Press them into the ground, wherever you are. [short pause] Feel the stability. [short pause] The weight of your body held by the floor beneath you. [short pause] These are feet fitted with the readiness that comes from the gospel of peace. [short pause] You are ready to go where you're needed today. [short pause] Not to fight people... [short pause] [short pause] to bring peace where there's conflict. [short pause] To carry calm into chaos.
Lord, make me a peacemaker. [short pause] Give me steady feet and a willingness to go where I'm sent.

Now the shield of faith. [short pause] Hold out your left hand, palm facing forward. [short pause] This is what stands between you and the flaming arrows... [short pause] [short pause] the sudden doubts, the whispered accusations, the fear that shows up without warning. [short pause] Not a shield of certainty. [short pause] A shield of trust. [short pause] You don't need to see the whole path. [short pause] You don't need to understand every detail. [short pause] Just the next step. [short pause] Faith is enough for that.
Lord, I trust you with what I cannot see and what I cannot control. [short pause] Strengthen my grip on this shield today.

The helmet of salvation. [short pause] Touch your forehead gently with your fingertips. [short pause] Feel the warmth of your own hand against your skin. [short pause] Your mind is protected. [short pause] The anxious spirals, the self-doubt, the comparison, the replaying of old failures... [short pause] [short pause] they hit the helmet, not your mind. [short pause] You get to choose which thoughts you entertain today and which ones you let pass.

Lord, guard my thinking. [short pause] When the lies come... [short pause] [short pause] and they will... [short pause] [short pause] help me recognize them for what they are. [short pause] And help me choose truth instead.

And finally... [short pause] [short pause] the sword of the Spirit, which is the Word of God. [short pause] Your right hand. [short pause] Close it gently, as if gripping a handle. [short pause] This is your one offensive weapon. [short pause] Not against people... [short pause] [short pause] never against people... [short pause] [short pause] but against the lies that say you're not enough, that today will overwhelm you, that you are alone in this fight.
Lord, make your Word alive in me today. [short pause] Bring the right verse to mind at the right moment. [short pause] And give me the courage to speak it.

Take one more deep breath. [short pause] Feel the full weight of what you're now carrying. [short pause] Truth around your center. [short pause] Righteousness on your chest. [short pause] Peace under your feet. [short pause] Faith in your hand. [short pause] Salvation on your mind. [short pause] The Word at your side.

Now picture yourself walking out of this room... [short pause] [short pause] armored. [short pause] Not stiff, not tense... [short pause] [short pause] but ready. [short pause] Walking into the hallway, the commute, the office, the kitchen, the conversation you've been avoiding... [short pause] [short pause] and every piece is with you. [short pause] Not because you're strong enough to carry it all. [short pause] But because God equipped you for exactly this day. [short pause] He knew what was coming before you did, and he made sure you had what you needed.
"Be strong in the Lord and in his mighty power."
Go in his strength. [short pause] You are equipped for whatever comes.
